sutta: the variable sarikuta said to the monks proven says when he that teaches wishes to teach another let him make 5 things stand up within him then let him teach another what 5 i will speak timely not untimely i will speak about what has happened not 1 has not i will speak with gentleness not harshness i will speak about what is beneficial not about what is not beneficial i will speak with mind of good will not with ill will provinces when he that teaches wishes to teach another let him make himself let him make these 5 things stand up in himself then let him teach another and just stop here for a while and just to commend these 5 things are the 5 characteristics of good speech first 1 you know you speak timely not untimely second 1 you speak the truth not what is false third 1 speak with gentleness speak gently not harshly not loudly fourth 1 speak on what is beneficial to that person if 1 speak with the mind of good will with a lot of good will then to continue the suture the variable sarikuta said baby sirs i see some monk being taught untimely moved not timely taught about what has not happened moved not about what has happened taught in a harsh way moved not with gentleness taught about what is not beneficial not about the beneficial thought with ill will moved not by good will in him not taught according to dhamma on 5 scores no remorse ought to be set up let him think this valuable sir has thought untimely not timely there's no need for remorse in me he has taught about what has not happened not what has has taught harshly not with gentleness has taught on what is not beneficial not on what is beneficial he has taught with ill will not with good will there is no need for remorse in me references in him that taught according to dhamma in him not taught according to dhamma on these 5 scores no remorse ought to be set up references in him that thought not according to dhamma on 5 scores odd remorse to be set up let him think untimely is the good man taught by me there's need for remorse in me he is taught by me about what has not happened not what has is harshly taught by me not gently is taught by me on what is not beneficial not what is beneficial is taught by me with ill will not with good will there is need for remorse in me references in him that taught not according to dhamma on him on these 5 scores all remorse to be set up why so that no monk should think 1 ought to be taught about what has not happened not beneficial etc there ought to be supports in 2 things for a person taught namely in truth and in the immovable if others teach me whether timely or not about what has happened or not gently or harshly about the beneficial or not with goodwill or with ill will i should find support in 2 things in truth and in the immovable and so the ambulator i should find support in 2 things in truth and in the immovable if i know it is in me this dhamma thinking it is i should declare it saying holy found in me is this dhamma but if i know it is not in me this dhamma thinking it is not i should declare that saying holy not found in me is this dhamma i stopped here for a while just now remember sarikuta said now we should find support in 2 things in truth and in the immovable immovable i suppose is like a state of equanimity equanimity improved meaning we should be honest and the other 1 whether we are taught rightly and we are taught wrongly we should be equanimous [Music] then the exalted 1 said and while you are thus speaking sarikuta i suppose some foolish men have not the talent to grasp the matter and very much sariputta replied lord those persons unbelievers who as a means of living not of faith go forth from the home to the homeless life imposters frauds deceivers who are vainly puffed up shifty gossipers of idol talk unguarded as to their sense doors eating without moderation unwakeful who seek not true reclusion are without diligence in training luxurious legs taking the lead in backsliding who cast aside the burden of the secluded life lazy low in energy not mindful not self-possessed with uncomposed and wondering minds dull language they while i speak in this way have not the talent to grasp the matter but those clansmen lord who of faith go forth from the home to the homeless life no imposters frauds deceivers not vainly puffed up not shifty gossipers of idol talk who are guarded as to as to their sense doors moderate and eating wakeful who seek true reclusion are diligent in training neither the luxurious nor legs who do not take the lead in backsliding eagles for seclusion with energy bestow themselves are resolved mindful self-possessed composed 1-pointed wise no language they while i speak in this way have the talent to grasp the matter and the buddha said that be those unbelieving etc etc foolish language sariputta speak you to those who of faith go forth admonish thy companions in the holy life sariputta instruct them and think then when i have stirred them up from what is not sadhama through dhamma i will make them stand fast in saddam true dhamma apparently sarikuta trained myself in this way so this last part sariputta is talking about the fraud monks and true monks is a long description of the fraud monks and those who go forth and all out of a sincere wish to cultivate the holy path and he calls them imposters frauds deceivers person unguarded as to the sense doors eating without moderation unwakeful not diligent in training likes luxurious would cast aside the burden of the secluded life lazy not mindful and composed with wandering minds it's a long description so from here you can see whether that's the way to judge a true monk from a false monk and next to ties
